meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no design skills (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome no design skills. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS project.
content.answer_block: You don't need a design degree to build beautiful apps. By leveraging Antigravity for UI scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can translate technical logic into polished interfaces automatically. This workflow bridges the gap between raw code and professional aesthetics, allowing you to focus on shipping functional software.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank code editor, the cursor blinking rhythmically, mocking your lack of design intuition. You have the logic, the backend, and the vision, but your UI looks like a relic from 1995. The fear of being 'just a coder' who can't ship a polished product is the single biggest barrier for students entering the SaaS space.

But the era of the 'designer-developer' divide is over. With the fusion of Git-integrated AI workflows, you can now treat design as a programmable asset. We are going to transform your raw functionality into a high-converting interface using nothing but intent and the right orchestration tools.

steps.title: Establish the Design System
steps.description: Before writing logic, define your visual constraints to ensure consistency across your app.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modern, minimalist design system for a SaaS dashboard. Include color palette (Tailwind format), typography scale, and standard button variants.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project scaffolding based on the Antigravity design system specs.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Layout Components
steps.description: Generate structural components to hold your application data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive sidebar layout with a header and main content area using Tailwind CSS and React.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create index.tsx with the generated responsive sidebar layout.'

steps.title: Iterative Logic Injection
steps.description: Feed your functional requirements into the UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the data structure for user profiles to the dashboard component generated in step 2.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the component to accept dynamic user data props.'

steps.title: Style Refinement via Context
steps.description: Use AI to tweak specific design elements that feel off without manual CSS hacking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current UI. Suggest 3 improvements for better visual hierarchy and whitespace.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the suggested visual hierarchy improvements to the main dashboard module.'

steps.title: Automated Version Control
steps.description: Commit your design progress to Git to create a history of your UI evolution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a descriptive Git commit message following conventional commits standards for the UI changes made.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'feat: implement responsive dashboard layout using AI-assisted design'

pro_tips: Always define your CSS variables in a single root file to allow AI to 'theme' your entire app with one prompt.
Use Claude Code to audit your code for accessibility (A11y) to ensure your design isn't just pretty, but functional for everyone.
When the AI suggests a design, ask it to explain the 'why' behind the spacing or color choice—this is how you learn design intuition.
Keep your components small and modular; it makes it easier for AI to rewrite specific UI sections without breaking the whole app.
Use Git branches for every major design experiment so you can roll back instantly if the AI drifts from your aesthetic vision.
content.next_steps: After completing this, try building a secondary page using the same design system to test the scalability of your UI components.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'I'm not a designer' excuse hold your portfolio back. You have the tools, the workflow, and the roadmap. Now, it is time to turn those prototypes into a live, production-ready SaaS application that proves your skills to the world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
